subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: governance/rights/politics from independent sources (IMF, World Bank, V-Dem, Freedom House, RSF, TI, HRW, Amnesty) + international press; Equatoguinean state media EXCLUDED there.

Teodoro Obiang (in power since 1979) is the world's longest-serving non-royal head of state - a closed, repressive system with no independent media, documented Black Beach prison torture, and the worst CPI of the regional petrostates (13/100).

Standout drags: extreme family capture of oil wealth (the Teodorin seizures, the 2024 Engonga embezzlement scandal), the paradox of plenty (top-tier per-capita wealth alongside majority poverty), and a sharp 2025 oil-led contraction; the CFA euro peg is the lone offset.

economic.notes: A peg-anchored but acutely declining-oil rentier on an IMF staff-monitored program.

fiscal_state.notes: A sharp 2025 contraction (~-6.4%) on falling hydrocarbons, on an IMF Staff-Monitored Program, with a long oil-depletion path and a weak non-oil base. (IMF 2025 Art IV/SMP; World Bank 2025)

monetary_independence.notes: The CEMAC CFA euro peg with a French convertibility guarantee - stable, non-sovereign. (BEAC; IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The CFA franc is regional, not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Low and peg-anchored near the CEMAC norm. (IMF)

banking_stability.notes: A COBAC-supervised CEMAC member - opaque, shallow, oil-exposed. (BEAC; IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Near-total hydrocarbon dependence with structurally declining output and negligible diversification - an acute resource curse. (IMF 2025; World Bank)

institutional.notes: Among the world's most corrupt and opaque states, with no independent media.

rule_of_law.notes: Among the world's most corrupt (CPI 13/100), no judicial independence, oil wealth captured by the ruling family (the Teodorin seizures; the 2024 Engonga conviction). (TI 2024; Freedom House 2025)

statistical_integrity.notes: Among the most opaque states - some IMF engagement under the SMP but data thin and unreliable. (IMF; World Bank)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Patronage-saturated and family-dominated despite oil rents. (World Bank)

press_freedom.notes: No independent media; pervasive censorship and journalist detention, among the world's worst. (RSF; Freedom House 2025)

political_social.notes: Suppressed stability under the world's longest-serving non-royal leader, with extreme inequality.

political_stability.notes: Suppressed surface stability under a leader in power since 1979 (~97% in 2022), with an acute, unmanaged eventual-succession risk inside the ruling family. (Freedom House 2025)

civil_liberties.notes: 'Not Free' - routine arbitrary arrest and documented torture at Black Beach prison. (Freedom House 2025; HRW; Amnesty 2024)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Closed and arbitrary toward foreigners, with a history of expelling West African migrants and exit/detention risk. (HRW)

social_cohesion.notes: Extreme inequality - top per-capita wealth alongside majority poverty - plus Bioko/mainland and ethnic cleavages. (World Bank 2025; HRW)

demographics.notes: Young and small-population with a nominal dividend, undercut by repression-driven exile. (World Bank; inference)

geopolitical.notes: A transactional hedger with targeted Western actions against the ruling family.

alliance_reliability.notes: Transactional hedging across the US (oil majors), China, Russia, and Spain; no firm bloc. (press; AidData)

conflict_involvement.notes: No active war; periodic coup-plot/mercenary scares but no open conflict. (press; ACLED)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Not broadly sanctioned, but targeted Western asset actions against the ruling family (Teodorin); CEMAC FX controls and opacity raise transfer risk. (US/French courts; IMF; BEAC)

physical_practical.notes: Weak outcomes despite top per-capita wealth - a hallmark of the diverted-rents model.

climate.notes: A low-readiness oil state with coastal-flood exposure and weak adaptive institutions despite income. (ND-GAIN; WB)

healthcare.notes: Weak outcomes despite top per-capita wealth - the diverted-rents model; a thin public system. (World Bank/WHO; HRW)

infrastructure.notes: Oil-funded showcase projects (Malabo, Ciudad de la Paz) above the poorest peers but unevenly distributed. (World Bank)

crime_safety.notes: A heavy security-state presence, with arbitrary-detention risk the larger personal hazard than street crime. (OSAC; HRW)

summary: The most closed and repressive of the Central African petrostates: Obiang has ruled since 1979 (the world's longest-serving non-royal head of state), oil wealth is captured by his family, and the state scores among the world's worst on corruption (CPI 13/100) and press freedom, with routine torture documented at Black Beach prison. It embodies the paradox of plenty - among the highest GDP-per-capita in sub-Saharan Africa alongside majority poverty - now compounded by a sharp 2025 contraction (~-6.4%) as hydrocarbons decline, with the country on an IMF Staff-Monitored Program. The 2024 Engonga scandal offered a rare window into elite rot. The CFA euro peg and low inflation are the only structural positives; the skew is sharply negative, with eventual family succession the dominant unresolved tail risk.
